Ruranand and Suryanand.

In the 'Gangoli Sakuri' root, who are the three sons of Taranand?
Jaykrishna, Baidyanath, and Jagannath.

Padmanand, Chandranand, Dharani, and Sitaram.

Ramkrishna, Gaurikant, and Kalikant.

Who are the three sons of Mahesh in the Pothia village branch?

Dharmanath, Chandranath, Dayanath, and Buddhinath.
15229

It refers to the ancestral origin point or the primary lineage base of a 
family.
15230

It indicates the specific village or current place of residence for a 
family branch.

_____.
Son

Pothia

Yashodhar

Sundarkant

_____.
Suryakant

Vipin

Shrikrishna

Vinodanand and Panchanand.

Kamlakant.

Madhukant.

Sodarpur.
15872
What specific marital detail is noted for the 'Tanti' lineage in entry 
48?
Marriage into the Tanti caste (Tanti Jati me Vivah).
15873
Entry 49 records a connection to the _____ community, which is 
traditionally associated with leather work.
Chammakarini.
15874
In entry 50, which village is associated with the 'Iraun' lineage?
Mandar.
15875
Entry 51 links the Sodarpur Pali lineage with the 'Khandwala' 
village and which other specific site?
Dih.
15876
The 'Hanidani Ruramati' lineage in entry 52 is associated with 
which village group?
Sodarpur Mandar.
15877
Which female figure's daughter is mentioned in the inter-caste 
context of entry 54?
Rajakeshwar Suta (Daughter of Rajakeshwar).
15878
What specific community does the term 'Rajak' refer to in entries 54 
and 55?
The washerman community.
15879
In entry 56, the Sodarpur Karmaha lineage is noted for a marriage 
with a daughter from the _____ community.
Gopa (Yadav).
15880
Which village is linked to the 'Belauch' lineage in entry 57?
Sodarpur.
15881
In entry 58, the 'Hariam' lineage is recorded alongside which village 
associated with 'Madhav'?
Darihara.
15882
Which village is associated with the 'Ghusaut' lineage in entry 59?
Sodarpur.
15883
Entry 60 records an inter-caste marriage involving a daughter of the 
_____ community from Naharua.
Chammakar (Leather worker).
15884
The 'Tailika Devai' mention in entry 61 refers to a marriage into 
which community?
The Teli (oil-presser) community.
15885
In entry 62, the 'Karmauli' lineage is associated with a marriage into 
the _____ family.
Mahapatra.
15886
Which community is again mentioned in entry 63 as being involved 
in an 'Antarjatiya' union?
Chammakar (Naharua).
15887
Entry 64 notes a 'Rajak Parampara' (washerman tradition) in the 
context of the _____ lineage.
Darihara Karmaha.
15888
In entry 66, the wife of Shakti is noted to be from the _____ 
community.
Champo Yavani.
15889
Which lineage is linked to 'Jhanjharpur' in entry 67?
Udaypur.
15890
In entry 68, the 'Budhwal' lineage is associated with which village 
group?
Pavoli Satlakha.
15891
Entry 69 links the 'Sodarpur Karmaha' group with which village and 
the individual 'Gadadhar'?
Mandar.
15892
Which lineage is associated with the 'Yamugam' village in entry 70? Sodarpur Satlakha.
15893
The 'Valiyas Gangoli' group in entry 71 is linked to which village?
Vithual.
15894
In entry 72, 'Sodarpur Budhwal' is grouped with which specific 
location?
Jagath.
15895
Which individual is associated with the 'Sodarpur Baheradhi' 
lineage in entry 73?
Shrikar.
15896
What specific caste is mentioned in the 'Darbhanga Shakha' 
(Darbhanga branch) in entry 74?
Yadav (Jaisingh).
15897
Entry 75 mentions 'Yadav Rahmu' in the context of which lineage?
Khandwala.
15898
Which village is linked to the 'Pandua Hariam' lineage in entry 76?
Mandar.
15899
In entry 77, the 'Sodarpur Karmaha' lineage is associated with 
which 'Ashram'?
Simhashram.
15900
Entry 78 records a marriage involving the 'Naharua Chammakarini' 
and which individual?
Giru.
15901
The term 'Param Brahman' in entry 79 is applied to which status or 
title?
Mahapatra.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
15902
In entry 80, the 'Sodarpur Baheradhi' lineage is linked to which 
village group?
Sodarpur Chakliya.
15903
The individuals 'Harhu' and 'Raghu' in entry 33 belong to which 
lineage?
Sodarpur Darihara.
15904
In entry 34, 'Lakhan' and 'Janardan' are descendants of which 
lineage?
Sodarpur Mandar.
15905
Which lineage is linked with 'Sakraudhi' in entry 35?
Sodarpur Satlakha.
15906
The name 'Aravind' in entries 35-37 is frequently associated with 
the label _____.
Nishiddha Kula (Prohibited lineage).
15907
Entry 37 lists 'Haridatt' and 'Vagishwar' under which primary 
lineage?
Sarisav Karmaha.
15908
In entry 39, 'Basau' and 'Mahai' are associated with which village?
Sodarpur Pali.
15909
Which lineage is linked to 'Udaypur' in entry 41?
Hariam.
15910
Entry 42 mentions 'Gadadhar' and 'Ramnath' in relation to which 
lineage?
Sodarpur Karmaha.
15911
Which village is associated with the 'Gangoli' lineage in entry 45?
Navaranga.
15912
Entry 46 links 'Shankar' and 'Veni' to the 'Sodarpur Hariam' group 
and which village?
Mandar.
15913
In entry 47, 'Matkar' and 'Bhmaru' are associated with which 
lineage?
Sodarpur Valias.
15914
The 'Tanti' lineage in entry 48 is also linked to which village?
Sodarpur.
15915
Which lineage is associated with 'Dadi' and 'Tattvachintamani' in 
entry 49?
Charmakarini.
15916
In entry 52, 'Dalu' and 'Gadu' are individuals belonging to which 
group?
Hanidani Ruramati.
15917
Which village is linked to 'Iraun Mandar' in entry 53?
Jamuni.
15918
Entry 54 records 'Sadhukar' and 'Shrikar' under the _____ lineage.
Khaual Vaheradhi.
15919
In entry 55, the 'Khaual Vahoradhi' lineage is linked to a marriage 
with a daughter from the _____ caste.
Rajak (Washerman).
15920
Which lineage is associated with 'Vamdev' and 'Raghunath' in entry 
56?
Sodarpur Karmaha.
15921
In entry 57, 'Guni' and 'Shankar' are linked to which village?
Sodarpur Belauch.
15922
Entry 59 mentions 'Ram' and 'Krishnadev' in the context of which 
group?
Sodarpur Ghusaut.
15923
Which individual is linked to the 'Charmakar Naharua' community 
in entry 60?
Nagar.
15924
In entry 61, 'Mahipati' and 'Kanh' are associated with the _____ 
village group.
Khandwala Thataunt.
15925
Entry 63 links 'Shiru' and 'Matu' with the 'Budhwal' lineage and 
which other location?
Sakraudhi Pali.
15926
Which lineage is linked to 'Someshwar' and 'Sureshwar' in entry 65? Rajak Parampara (washerman tradition).
15927
In entry 66, 'Krishnapathi' and 'Suryapathi' are associated with the 
_____ lineage.
Mandar Pachahi.
15928
Entry 67 links 'Bhave' and 'Raghupati' to the 'Rajak' tradition and 
which village?
Sodarpur Hariam.
15929
Which individuals are linked to the 'Sodarpur Karmaha' group in 
entry 69?
Gadadhar and Visho.
15930
In entry 70, 'Harikesh' and 'Kashi' are associated with the _____ 
lineage.
Sodarpur Satlakha.
15931
Entry 74 mentions 'Shivapathi' and 'Yagyapathi' in the context of 
the _____ community.
Yadav.
15932
Which individual is named as a 'MahapÄ tra' (Param Brahmin) in 
entry 79?
Hore.
15933
Which village is linked to the 'Khaual' lineage in entry 79?
Darihara.
15934
In entry 75, the wife of Hrishikesh is noted as being from the _____ 
caste.
Yadav.
15935
Which individual is linked to 'Gadhapandoli' in entry 36?
Ratir Siddhu.
15936
What is the status of the 'maternal unknown' record for JÅ«á¸ 
ÄuÃ± 
in entry 33?
Matruka Agyat.
15937
The 'Darihara' village is linked to the 'Ghusaut' lineage in which 
numbered entry?
33
15938
What is the English translation of the title 'Dushana Panji' used in 
the source material?
The Black Book
15939
Who is the author of the provided excerpts from 'Dushana Panji'?
Gajendra Thakur

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
15940
In the context of Maithil Brahmin genealogy, what does the term 
'Dushana' signify?
A blemish or social/ritual fault within a lineage, typically resulting 
from an irregular marriage.
15941
According to the 'Vyakhya' (Explanation) section, how many 
generations does it typically take for a 'Dushana' lineage to become 
'Shuddha' (pure)?
Six generations (attaining purity in the sixth generation).
15942
Which historical figure's lineage is used as an example of reaching 
'Shuddha' status in the sixth generation?
Devdhar
15943
What specific social fault is associated with Bhatt Bhushan of the 
Mandar Marar lineage in entry 83?
Marriage with a 'Guru-patni' (a teacher's wife).
15944
The entry for Afel (Entry 87) records a 'Dushana' involving 
marriage with a woman from which background?
A 'Yavani' (foreigner or Muslim woman), specifically named 
Daulat Jahan.
15945
What is the name of the 'Yavani' woman mentioned in the blemish 
involving the Sodarpur Khaual lineage?
Daulat Jahan
15946
In entry 94, what type of woman is 'Vartini', leading to a lineage 
blemish?
A widow ('Vartini Vidhwa').
15947
According to entry 96, what was the status of the woman 'Dheevar 
Dasi' who married into the Kujouli Mandar lineage?
A slave or servant from the fisherman caste.
15948
What does the term 'Path-patita kanya' refer to in the record of 
blemishes?
A girl who has fallen from her social or moral path/caste status.
15949
Which philosopher, known for writing the 'Tattva-Chintamani', is 
mentioned in a 'Dushana' involving a 'Chamarkarini' (leatherworker 
woman)?
Gangesha (Gangesh Upadhyaya).
15950
According to the text, how many years after his father's death was 
Gangesha allegedly born?
Five years.
15951
What is a 'Mool' in the context of these genealogical records?
An ancestral village or the root lineage identity of a Maithil 
Brahmin.
15952
List three examples of Maithil Brahmin 'Mools' mentioned in the 
text.
Sodarpur, Mandar, and Karmaha.
15953
The 'Dushana' involving 'Gop-vivah' (Entry 97) refers to marriage 
with a woman from which caste?
The Gop (cowherd) caste.
15954
What is the 'Dushana' associated with the 'Koch Rajvanshi' kings of 
Assam in these records?
Lineage blemishes resulting from marriages or associations with the 
Koch royal family.
15955
In entry 85, a blemish is recorded for a marriage with a girl 
described as _____.
Path-patita kanya (a fallen girl).
15956
The term 'Agyat' often appears in the mother's column in the Panji; 
what does this indicate?
The identity or lineage of the mother is unknown or not recorded.
15957
Which lineage is associated with the 'Khandavala' Mool in entry 82? Khandavala Karmaha.
15958
Entry 90 mentions people arriving from which direction as a source 
of lineage concern?
The West ('Paschim desh-sa agat').
15959
What marker or sign ('Chinha') is used in the Panji to denote a 
'Param Brahmin' (Maha-pataka) daughter's offspring?
A specific symbol or 'Chinha' is placed in the records to identify 
them as descendants of a 'great sinner'.
15960
Who is the son of Gangu in the Baheradhi lineage, mentioned in the 
explanation section?
Do.
15961
In the Sodarpur lineage, Parashurama is the son of which 
individual?
Govinda.
15962
The blemish involving 'Rajak Kanya' (Entry 26) refers to a woman 
from which profession/caste?
Washerman (Rajak).
15963
The 'Dushana' involving 'Chamarkarini' (Leatherworker) is 
associated with which name in the explanation?
Ananda.
15964
Which text is Gangesha credited with writing in the genealogical 
'Vyakhya'?
Tattva-Chintamani.
15965
According to the list, what is the Mool of the person named Jasu in 
entry 86?
Sodarpur Khoual.
15966
Which entry number corresponds to the 'Kerwal Shakha' (Kerwal 
branch)?
Entry 92.
15967
In the records, what does 'Mataui' likely refer to in the context of 
village lists?
A specific maternal village or sub-location associated with the 
lineage.
15968
The blemish involving the 'Mandar' Mool and 'Sawai's son Surasar' 
is categorized under which type of irregular marriage?
It is listed as a specific 'Chinha' (mark) of blemish in the 
explanation notes.
15969
Name the individual from the 'Kujouli' Mool who married a 
'Dheevar Dasi'.
Mandal.
15970
In entry 95, the lineage 'Hariam' is associated with which primary 
Mool?
Sodarpur.
15971
Which lineage is linked to the location 'Sarisav' in entry 90?
Karmaha.
15972
The name 'Daulat Jahan' represents a blemish of what 
religious/ethnic nature in this Brahmin record?
Yavani (Muslim).
15973
What is the father's name of the person 'Gangadhar' mentioned in 
the explanation of the first example?
Camru (of the Valiyas-mool).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
15974
To which Mool does the person 'Goge' belong in the purification 
example?
Sakrarhi.
15975
The purification of the lineage was complete when Devdhar's 
mother was from which Mool?
Irawal (or Karwal).
15976
In entry 88, which Mool is mentioned alongside 'Mahishi' and 
'Budhwal'?
Sodarpur.
15977
The term 'Maha-pataka' used in the notes refers to what?
A 'great sin' or a person who has committed a severe social/religious 
transgression.
15978
Which 'Mool' is associated with entry 216/I (Entry 86)?
Sodarpur Khoual.
15979
What is the significance of the village 'Darihara' in these records?
It is one of the frequently cited 'Mools' or villages where various 
lineages are based.
15980
The blemish associated with 'Asamaka Koch Rajvanshi' is linked to 
a daughter named _____.
Sohago.
15981
In the explanation, the marriage of 'Jivadhar's son Gahai' is linked 
to which Mool?
Tankbal.
15982
Which individual from the 'Karmaha' Mool married 'Harilata'?
Raghunath's son Harinath.
15983
The 'Vartini Vidhwa' (remarried widow) blemish in entry 94 is 
specifically linked to which Mool?
Pali.
15984
Who is the son of 'Sudarshan' mentioned in the Sakrarhi lineage 
context?
Madhav.
15985
The blemish involving a 'Rajak' (washerman) girl 'Runamathi' is 
associated with which person's descendants?
Hadir-i (or similar phonetic name in the text).
15986
In entry 89, which Mool is listed first in the sequence of 'Iroun', 
'Pali', and 'Darihara'?
Iroun (or Irauri).
15987
The name 'Sodarpur Belaunch' appears in which entry number?
Entry 85 (206/II).
15988
Which Mool is associated with 'Panchobh' and 'Pavauli' in entry 88? Mahishi.
15989
According to the text, the child of a 'Chamarkarini' (leatherworker) 
and 'Gangesha's father' was born how many years after the father's 
absence?
Five years.
15990
Which specific branch of the 'Maithil' Brahmins is the primary 
subject of the 'Dushana Panji'?
Those recorded in the Panji Prabandha (specifically the lineage 
records of the Mithila region).
15991
What is the status of the 'Shuddha' child in the final stage of the 
genealogical example?
Fully purified ('Purnatah Shuddha').
15992
Entry 91 lists 'Brahmapura' and 'Purahi' alongside which primary 
Mool?
Sodarpur Pali.
15993
The blemish 'Yadava Rahmuka Putre' involves a marriage with a 
woman from which community?
The Yadava community (cowherds/Gop).
15994
In entry 81, 'Budhwal Mandar' is associated with which specific 
location?
Kujouli.
15995
Who is the 'Gajendra Thakur' mentioned in the document footer?
The author or editor of this compilation of the 'Dushana Panji'.
15996
In entry 93, the 'Sodarpur Darihara' lineage is noted for a marriage 
with a girl from which background?
A 'Path-patita kanya' (fallen girl) from the Gangoli lineage.
15997
What is the primary purpose of the 'Dushana Panji' records?
To track and preserve the history of 'faults' or 'blemishes' in lineages 
to prevent ritually 'impure' marriages.
15998
The term 'Mataui' (à¤®à¤¤à¤¾à¤‰à¤¿) used in entries like 81 and 
84 likely refers to _____.
Maternal ancestry or the maternal village connection.
15999
Which 'Mool' is associated with the person 'Ratipati's son Balbhar'?
Sodarpur.
16000
The entry 227 (Entry 88) mentions 'Mahishi' as the primary 
location; which region is this historically part of?
Mithila.
16001
What 'Chinha' is specifically linked to the 'Asamaka Koch 
Rajvanshi' in the explanation?
The blemish mark associated with the daughter named Sohago.
16002
In entry 94, the 'Vartini Vidhwa' is described as the child of whom? Ganapati Chand.
16003
What is the historical period covered by the 'Genome Mapping' of 
the Maithil Panji Prabandh?
It covers the period from 450 AD to 2009 AD.
16004
What was the primary purpose for the creation of the Panji 
Prabandh during the medieval period?
To maintain the 'Jati Shuddhi' (caste purity) of Brahmins, 
Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
16005
In the context of Maithil genealogy, what is an 'Uteh'?
An introduction of seven generations of ancestors, requiring the 
identification of 32 specific family lines.
16006
What are the four specific pairs of great-grandparents required for a 
full 'Uteh' introduction?
Paternal Great-grandfather/Great-grandmother, Paternal Great-
grandparents of the mother, Maternal Great-grandfather/Great-
grandmother, and Maternal Great-grandparents of the mother.
16007
According to Panji rules, what is the required generation gap for 
marriage on the paternal side?
The bride and groom must be separated by at least seven 
generations (often revised to six in specific practices).
16008
What is the required generation gap for marriage on the maternal 
side in the Panji system?
The bride and groom must be separated by at least five generations.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16009
Which historical ruler is credited with the formal organisation and 
institutionalisation of the Panji Prabandh?
Maharaja Hari Singh Dev (Karnat dynasty).
16010
In which year (AD and Saka era) was the decision made to 
institutionalise the Panji records under state-appointed scholars?
1326 AD (Saka 1248).
16011
Who was the scholar appointed by Maharaja Hari Singh Dev to 
manage the Panji records for Maithil Brahmins?
Pandit Gunakar Jha of the Mandar-Pandua Mula.
16012
Who were the scholars appointed to manage the Panji records for 
Karna Kayasthas and Kshatriyas, respectively?
Shankardatt (for Kayasthas) and Vijaydatt (for Kshatriyas).
16013
How many total Gotras are recognised within the Maithil Brahmin 
community according to the Panji?
20 Gotras.
16014
What is the difference between 'Tri-pravar' and 'Pancha-pravar'?
Tri-pravar refers to Gotras with three Rigvedic sage ancestors, 
while Pancha-pravar refers to those with five.
16015
Which three Gotras are classified as 'Pancha-pravar'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj.
16016
In Maithil genealogy, what does the term 'Beji Purush' represent?
The earliest known or founding ancestor of a specific family lineage 
or 'Mula'.
16017
What is the difference between a 'Mula' and a 'Mula-Gram'?
Mula is the original ancestral root, while Mula-Gram is the specific 
village of residence at the time the Panji was recorded.
16018
What are the two primary Vedic branches followed by Maithil 
Brahmins?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina Shakha) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
Shakha).
16019
What is the specific title given to Samavedi Maithil Brahmins in the 
Panji system?
Chhandogya.
16020
What is the specific title given to Yajurvedi Maithil Brahmins in the 
Panji system?
Vajasaneyi.
16021
How does the Chhandogya marriage ritual differ from the 
Vajasaneyi ritual regarding timing?
Chhandogya weddings occur in two parts (Purva and Uttara) with 
the latter after sunset, whereas Vajasaneyi weddings have no such 
division.
16022
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (also known as Aswajana Patra)?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride 
and groom are eligible to marry and are not 'Swajana' (forbidden 
relatives).
16023
The list of Maithil Brahmin classifications includes Shrotriya, 
Yogya, Panjibaddh, and _____.
Jaibar.
16024
In the Panji hierarchy, which category of Brahmins is also referred 
to as 'Pachhwari-paat'?
Those other than the Shrotriya (primarily the Panjibaddh and 
Yogya).
16025
According to the 'Tanra Varttik' by Kumarila Bhatta, what was the 
'Samuha-lekhya' used for?
To record the lineage of noble families to ensure their qualities and 
flaws were remembered for future marriages.
16026
What is the 'Chhatth' in the context of marriage eligibility 
calculation?
It refers to the 16 specific ancestral positions (paternal and 
maternal) from which a person's 6th generation status is calculated.
16027
Who is the 1st 'Chhatth' ancestor used to determine a girl's marriage 
eligibility?
The Great-grandfather's Grandfather (Vriddha-prapitamaha ke 
pitamaha).
16028
What is the rule regarding a groom's mother's brother in the Panji 
system?
A groom cannot marry a girl who is the child of his mother's 
brother.
16029
How many primary 'Mulas' were originally identified, and which 
branch was added later as the 35th?
167 were identified; the Phandah Mula's Kharaur branch was added 
as the 35th.
16030
Identify the first three Gotras in the standard list of 20.
Shandilya, Vatsa, and Savarna.
16031
Which script is traditionally used to write the Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
16032
What are the three main categories of the primary 35 Mulas?
Ayanthi Shrestha (First Class), Dwitiya Shreni (Second Class), and 
Madhyam (Middle Class).
16033
According to the Panji preservation rule 'Jalat Raksha Tailat 
Raksha', what is the third thing one should protect books from?
Loose or weak binding (Sthul Bandhanat).
16034
During which month/zodiac transition is it recommended to dry 
Panji books in the sun?
When the sun is in Leo (Sinha Rashi), roughly from 16th August to 
15th September.
16035
What are the seven types of Panji described in the text?
Mula, Shakha, Gotra, Panji, Dushan, Uteh, and Aswajanya-patra 
Panji.
16036
Which Panji record specifically notes flaws or 'kshana' that have 
entered a lineage?
Dushan Panji.
16037
The 'Behat-Kimaha' branch of the Shandilya Gotra is a notable 
exception because most Kimaha belong to the _____ Gotra.
Vatsa
16038
In the classification of Panji, how many total 'Panjis' exist for the 
Shrotriya and Panjibaddh/Yogya categories?
185 total; 32 for Shrotriya and 153 for Yogya/Panjibaddh.
16039
What is the status of Panji records for the Kshatriya community in 
the modern era?
They have largely become extinct (Vilupt).
16040
Who is the 'Beji Purush' of the Mandar Mula established roughly in 
400 AD?
Mahamahopadhyaya Trivenir Bhatta.
16041
Which famous poet and scholar's lineage is traceable through the 
Shakha Panji?
Vidyapati Thakur.
16042
What specific genealogical 'purity' issue occurred with Pandit Hari 
Nath Upadhyay that necessitated the formalisation of Panji?
He unknowingly married his own cousin (his father's sister's 
daughter's daughter) due to a lack of recorded lineage.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16043
In the 'Pancha Gauda' classification of Brahmins, which five 
regions are included?
Utkal, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
16044
In the 'Pancha Dravid' classification of Brahmins, which five 
regions are included?
Karnat, Gurjar, Tailang, Dravid, and Maharashtra.
16045
Under which Maharaja did the 'Shakha Panji' and the classification 
into Shrotriya start?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
16046
Which scholar is known for the lineage of 'Modanand Jha' and 
'Vidyanand Jha' spanning 19 generations?
Gunakar Jha.
16047
Concept: Gotra
Definition: A paternal lineage tracking descent from a specific 
ancient sage (Rishi).
16048
Concept: Pravar
Definition: The specific subset of distinguished ancestors within a 
Gotra who were composers of Vedic hymns.
16049
How does the Panji determine 'Mula' for a person living in a new 
village?
The 'Mula' is fixed as the earliest known ancestral village, while the 
current residence becomes the 'Mula-Gram'.
16050
What is the genealogical position known as 'Dauhitra'?
A grandson through one's daughter.
16051
True or False: According to the Panji, marriage between Vatsa and 
Savarna Gotras is permitted because they are different Gotras.
False; they share the same ancestors and marriage between them is 
prohibited.
16052
What is the social consequence of marrying a 'Swajana' (forbidden 
relative) according to ancient Smritis cited in the text?
The offspring are considered 'Chandala' and the parents are 
outcasted from Arya dignity.
16053
Which Gotra includes Mulas like Dighey, Sarisab, Mahua, and Pali? Shandilya.
16054
Which Gotra includes Mulas like Oini, Khauaal, Mandar, and 
Visphi?
Kashyap.
16055
The 11th 'Chhatth' used for checking eligibility is the maternal 
grandfather's _____.
Prapitamaha (Great-grandfather).
16056
The 16th 'Chhatth' used for checking eligibility is the maternal 
grandmother's _____.
Matru-matamaha (Maternal great-grandfather).
16057
What was the role of 'Panjikars' appointed by royal decree?
To verify lineages and issue 'Siddhanta' certificates ensuring no 
'Swajana' marriage occurs.
16058
Which Mula branch is historically associated with the poet 
Vidyapati?
Bisphi (Kashyap Gotra).
16059
What is the primary difference in the Upanayana (sacred thread) 
ceremony between Vajasaneyi and Chhandogya Brahmins?
Vajasaneyi includes 'Ved-arambh' as a distinct sanskar, which is 
not counted separately in Chhandogya.
16060
In the Panji hierarchy, 'Panjibaddh' people are those whose names 
were officially entered into the _____.
Panji (genealogical registers).
16061
The Panji system records not just ancestry but also historical details 
like educational titles and _____.
Migrations (Pravas-Apravas).
16062
What did Maharaja Kameshwar Singh award to genealogists who 
passed the 'Dhaut Pariksha'?
A 'Doshala' (shawl) and titles like 'Panji-Shastra Martand'.
16063
How does the Panji track individuals who migrated to distant lands 
like Bengal or Assam?
They are recorded as 'Deshantar-vasi' or associated with the new 
village while retaining their original 'Mula'.
16064
The Mula 'Bramhapura' is unique because it is found in _____ 
different Gotras.
Six (Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyap, Alambukaksh, Gautam, and 
Bharve).
16065
Which Panji is used specifically to understand the 'Apapatya' 
(descendants) of a line?
Par-Panji.
16066
True or False: The Panji system allows for the recording of 
marriages with non-Brahmin or outcaste groups if they occur.
True; historical Panjis record such instances (e.g., marriages with 
'Chamarkarin' or 'Yavani') as a matter of factual record.
16067
What does 'Sthul Bandhanat' mean in the context of book 
preservation?
It refers to avoiding tight or heavy binding that could damage the 
palm-leaf or paper pages.
16068
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' system in 
Mithila?
To maintain caste purity and ensure the validity of marriages by 
verifying lineage.
16069
Under whose reign was the Panji Prabandh system formally 
organised in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
16070
In what year (Shaka and Gregorian) was the Panji Prabandh 
officially implemented?
Shaka 1248, which corresponds to 1326 AD.
16071
According to the Panji system, what are the two essential 
components of a Maithil Brahmin's identity?
Gotra and Moola.
16072
How many total 'Gotras' are recognised among Maithil Brahmins?
20
16073
Which seven Gotras are considered the most common and well-
established in Mithila?
Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, Bharadwaja, and 
Katyayana.
16074
Term: Gotra
Definition: A lineage system tracing descent from a specific ancient 
sage (Rishi).
16075
Term: Moolagram
Definition: The original village of residence of a lineage's founding 
ancestor.
16076
Term: Beji Purush
Definition: The earliest known founding ancestor of a specific 
family lineage.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16077
How does the Panji system define 'Pravara'?
A sub-classification of Gotra based on the specific ancestors who 
composed Rigvedic hymns.
16078
What is the difference between 'Tri-pravara' and 'Panch-pravara' 
families?
Tri-pravara identifies three hymn-composing ancestors, while 
Panch-pravara identifies five.
16079
Why is marriage prohibited between the 'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' 
Gotras?
They share the same hymn-composing ancestors (Pravaras), 
indicating a shared lineage.
16080
In the context of lineage verification, what does the term 'Uti' refer 
to?
The detailed introduction and verification of 32 specific ancestral 
lines.
16081
What is the maximum number of paternal generations that must be 
avoided to ensure marriage eligibility?
Seven generations (later modified to six in certain practices).
16082
What is the maximum number of maternal generations that must be 
avoided to ensure marriage eligibility?
Five generations.
16083
What was the total number of 'Moolas' (original family roots) 
initially recorded in the Panji system?
167
16084
How many 'Main Moolas' are traditionally identified within the 167 
original roots?
34 (later 35 including the Phandah branch).
16085
Who was the first scholar to mention the 'Samuh Lekhya' (family 
records) before the formal Panji system?
Kumarila Bhatt in his work 'Tantra Varttik'.
16086
Who was the specialist scholar appointed by Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Brahmin Panji?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
16087
Who was the scholar appointed to manage the Panji for the Karna 
Kayasthas?
Shankardatt.
16088
Who was the scholar appointed to manage the Panji for the 
Kshatriyas?
Vijayadatt.
16089
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Asvajana Patra)?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride 
and groom are not prohibited relatives.
16090
What is the title given to the professional genealogists who maintain 
and verify the Panji records?
Panjikar.
16091
Into which two major Vedic branches are Maithil Brahmins divided 
based on their ritual practices?
Chhandogya (Samaveda) and Vajasaneyi (Yajurveda).
16092
How do Chhandogya and Vajasaneyi Brahmins differ in their 
marriage rituals?
Chhandogya weddings are split into two parts (Purva and Uttara), 
whereas Vajasaneyi weddings have no such division.
16093
Which branch of Yajurveda is followed by the Vajasaneyi 
Brahmins of Mithila?
The Madhyandina Shakha.
16094
Which branch of Samaveda is followed by the Chhandogya 
Brahmins of Mithila?
The Kauthuma Shakha.
16095
Into how many categories are Maithil Brahmins broadly classified 
based on lineage purity?
Three main categories: Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
16096
What is the term for Brahmins whose names are not recorded in the 
formal Panji books?
Jaibar.
16097
How many 'Chaupala' (hierarchical categories) exist within the 
Shrotriya Panji?
Eight.
16098
How many specific Panji books are dedicated to the Shrotriya and 
Yogya/Panjibaddh classes respectively?
32 for Shrotriyas and 153 for Yogya/Panjibaddh classes.
16099
What is 'Laukiki' in the context of the Shrotriya class?
It refers to the specific Panji records tracking the Shrotriya lineage.
16100
What are the names of the two subgroups of Maithil Brahmins 
based on geographic location in later periods?
Pubari (Eastern) and Pachhabari (Western).
16101
Concept: Dushana Panji
A record of specific genealogical 'faults' or 'stains' in a family 
lineage to prevent their spread.
16102
Concept: Shakha Panji
A detailed family tree tracking descendants and their specific 
maternal lineages over several centuries.
16103
In the Panji system, what does 'Chhatth' (Sixth) signify in marriage 
eligibility?
The check of 16 specific ancestral positions to ensure a bride/groom 
is at least six degrees removed from common ancestors.
16104
According to the mnemonic for book preservation, what are the 
three main threats to Panji manuscripts?
Water, oil, and loose binding.
16105
During which astrological period (Sun in Leo) is it recommended to 
dry Panji manuscripts?
Roughly from 16 August to 15 September.
16106
Which Gotra is exclusively followed by the 'Karna Kayasthas' of 
Mithila?
Kashyapa.
16107
How is marriage eligibility determined for Karna Kayasthas, given 
they share a single Gotra?
Eligibility is based solely on the 'Moola' (ancestral root) and the 'Uti' 
(ancestral lineage).
16108
What does the 'Beji' of a Moola represent in a family's history?
The specific founding individual from whom that branch of the 
family originated.
16109
Why did Panji documentation cease among the Kshatriya 
community of Mithila?
Over time, the strict recording practice was lost, and they moved 
toward avoiding marriages within entire villages to prevent incest.
16110
What was the major change introduced during the reign of 
Maharaja Madhav Singh regarding Panjis?
The introduction of 'Shakha Panji' and the formalisation of 
hierarchies like Shrotriya.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16111
Which famous poet's genealogy is recorded in the Shakha Panjis for 
19 generations?
Vidyapati Thakur.
16112
According to the system of 'Pancha Gauda', which five Brahmin 
groups belong to North India?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
16113
According to the system of 'Pancha Dravida', which five Brahmin 
groups belong to South India?
Karnata, Gurjara, Tailanga, Dravida, and Maharashtra.
16114
What is the significance of the 'Vindhya Mountains' in Brahmin 
classification?
They serve as the geographic boundary between the Pancha Gauda 
(North) and Pancha Dravida (South) Brahmins.
16115
In the Panji terminology, what is a 'Swajana'?
A relative with whom marriage is prohibited due to close blood 
relation (Sapinda).
16116
What social consequence traditionally faced children born from 
prohibited 'Swajana' marriages?
They were historically categorised as 'Chandal' and excluded from 
Aryan social dignity.
16117
Name the scholars credited with the modern digitization and editing 
of the Panji Theory material.
Gajendra Thakur, Nagendra Kumar Jha, and Panjikar Vidyanand 
Jha.
16118
The Panji Prabandh is considered a medieval precursor to what 
modern biological concept?
Genome Mapping.
16119
According to the text, what is the 'Adharam' of Dharma in the 
context of Mithila's culture?
The purity of the 'Jati' (caste/lineage) through births from valid, non-
related parents.
16120
What is the rule regarding the 'Mother's brother's children' in 
Maithil Brahmin marriage?
Marriage with the offspring of the mother's brother is strictly 
prohibited.
16121
Term: Paanti
Definition: The social rank or 'line' indicating a family's standing 
within the Panji hierarchy.
16122
How does the Panji record handle families that migrate or change 
villages?
It tracks both the 'Moola' (original village) and the 'Deragaon' 
(current village of residence).
16123
Why is it considered ethical for a Panjikar to take disciples?
To ensure the survival of genealogical knowledge for future 
generations beyond just financial gain.
16124
What historical event led to the creation of the Panji system, 
involving a scholar named Harinath Upadhyaya?
He inadvertently married his first cousin (mother's sister's daughter) 
due to a lack of genealogical records.
16125
How many total ancestors are verified in the 'Uti' format for both 
the bride and groom?
32 for each (64 total).
16126
What is the 'Adho-gami' nature of the Panji status?
Status generally declines (moves downward) if one marries into a 
lower-ranked Panji family.
16127
In which year was the Panji Prabandh system officially established 
in Mithila?
1326 AD (Shaka 1248).
16128
Who was the Karnata dynasty king responsible for the formal 
implementation of the Panji Prabandh?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
16129
Which three primary social groups were initially covered by the 
Panji genealogical records?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
16130
Term: Uteá¸ h
Definition: The formal introduction of ancestors required to verify 
marriage eligibility.
16131
How many total ancestors must be identified in an Uteá¸ h to 
confirm a person's genealogical background?
32
16132
What is the primary purpose of the Panji Prabandh system?
To maintain caste purity and regulate marriage eligibility based on 
lineage.
16133
How many distinct Gotras are traditionally recognised among 
Maithil Brahmins?
20
16134
Term: Moola
Definition: The original village or ancestral root from which a 
family lineage is derived.
16135
How many 'Moola' (ancestral roots) were identified during the 
initial collection of Maithil Brahmin records?
167
16136
What was the designation for Maithil Brahmins who were not 
formally recorded in the Panji?
Jaybar.
16137
According to Panji rules, how many generations must be avoided 
on the paternal side for marriage?
Seven.
16138
According to Panji rules, how many generations must be avoided 
on the maternal side for marriage?
Five.
16139
Which two Maithil Brahmin Gotras are forbidden from 
intermarrying because they share the same ancestors?
Vatsa and Savarna.
16140
What are the two major Vedic branches followed by Maithil 
Brahmins?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina branch) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
branch).
16141
Maithil Brahmins following the White Yajurveda are known as 
_____.
Vajasaneyi
16142
Maithil Brahmins following the Samaveda are referred to as _____.
Chandoga
16143
Term: Tri-pravara
Definition: A Gotra lineage that claims descent from three specific 
Vedic sages.
16144
Term: Panch-pravara
Definition: A Gotra lineage that claims descent from five specific 
Vedic sages.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16145
Who was the scholar appointed as the first Panjikar for Maithil 
Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
16146
Who was the first person appointed to manage the Panji records for 
the Karna Kayasthas?
Shankardatta.
16147
Who was the first person appointed to manage the Panji records for 
the Kshatriyas?
Vijayadatta.
16148
Which later Maharaja of Darbhanga introduced the 'Shakha Panji' 
and created social hierarchies like Srotriya?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
16149
What is the function of a 'Siddhanta Patra'?
It is a formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a 
couple is eligible to marry.
16150
Term: Dushana Panji
Definition: A specific register used to record defects, blemishes, or 
violations of social norms within a lineage.
16151
In what script were the traditional Panji records primarily written?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
16152
What was the traditional physical material used for writing Panji 
records before the use of paper?
Palm leaves (Talpatra).
16153
Term: Biji Purusha
Definition: The foundational ancestor or the earliest known 
patriarch of a specific Moola (ancestral root).
16154
The five categories of Brahmins belonging to Northern India 
(including Maithils) are collectively known as _____.
Pancha Gauda
16155
What is the technical term for a scholar who specialises in 
maintaining and verifying the Panji records?
Panjikar.
16156
How does the Panji system identify a 'Srotriya' Brahmin?
By tracking a history of specific religious conduct and ancestral 
purity over several generations.
16157
The list of 16 specific ancestors (8 on the paternal side and 8 on the 
maternal side) used to check for overlapping lineages is called the 
_____.
Chhadvith (Sixteen ancestors)
16158
Which Gotra includes the Moolas of 'Dighwa', 'Sarisab', and 
'Mahua'?
Shandilya.
16159
Which Gotra includes the Moolas of 'Oini', 'Khoual', and 'Mandar'?
Kashyapa.
16160
What is the significance of the year 1326 AD in the context of 
Mithila's genealogy?
It marks the formal 'Panji Prabandh' event where existing lineages 
were recorded and codified.
16161
Term: Aswajanyapan
Definition: A state where a lineage becomes ineligible for certain 
social statuses due to marriage outside prescribed rules.
16162
In the Karna Kayastha community, how many distinct Gotras are 
traditionally followed?
Only one (Kashyapa).
16163
What does a 'Panji' record specifically track for each newborn in a 
family?
The name, the father's name, the mother's name, and the mother's 
ancestral Moola.
16164
Which Panji register focuses specifically on the 'Sapinda' (blood 
relative) restrictions?
Uteá¸ h Panji.
16165
How does the Panji system define a 'Yogya' Brahmin?
As a middle-tier classification between Srotriya and Panjibadh 
based on ancestral merit.
16166
What historical event in the 14th century prompted the creation of 
the Panji system?
A high-ranking scholar inadvertently married a close relative due to 
a lack of genealogical records.
16167
Concept: Grouping of Moolas
Definition: Moolas are often grouped by their Gotras; for instance, 
'Pali' and 'Hariamb' belong to the Vatsa Gotra.
16168
What is the 'Laukik' in the context of Srotriya Panji?
The specific social standing or 'fame' earned by a family through 
notable marriages or deeds.
16169
How are the Panji books traditionally protected from decay?
They are dried in the harsh sun when it is in the Leo sign (August-
September) and kept away from oil and water.
16170
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Ekhara' and 'Pali' Moolas in the 
list of minor Gotras?
Kaundinya.
16171
What is the primary difference between 'Tri-pravara' and 'Panch-
pravara' regarding Rishis?
Tri-pravara invokes three sages, while Panch-pravara invokes five 
during religious rituals.
16172
What was the significance of the work 'Tantra Vartika' by Kumarila 
Bhatta for genealogy?
It provided early philosophical justification for maintaining written 
ancestral records (Samuh-Lekhya).
16173
What is a 'Bramhapura' Moola noted for in the text?
It is a rare instance of a Moola that appears across multiple different 
Gotras.
16174
According to the Panji theory, birth alone does not ensure purity; it 
must be supported by _____.
Ancestral record verification (Panji)
16175
Who wrote 'Varna Ratnakara', which mentions the 72 Rajput clans 
relevant to the Panji era?
Jyotirishwar Thakur.
16176
The regional culture of Mithila was influenced by its geography, 
situated between the Himalayas and the _____ River.
Ganga
16177
What was the primary purpose of establishing the 'Panji Prabandh' 
in medieval Mithila?
To maintain the genealogical purity (Jati Shuddhi) of Brahmins, 
Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
16178
The introduction of seven generations of ancestors required for 
marriage eligibility is known as _____.
Uderh

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16179
How many total 'Gotras' (clans) are identified within the Maithil 
Brahmin community according to the Panji system?
$20$
16180
How many original villages or lineages, known as 'Mulgrams', were 
initially recorded in the Panji records?
$167$
16181
In the Maithil Brahmin hierarchy, what are the three main 
categories resulting from the Panji classification?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
16182
According to the Shastras, a 'Shrotriya' Brahmin must avoid how 
many generations of ancestors on the mother's side for marriage?
Five generations.
16183
According to the Shastras, a 'Shrotriya' Brahmin must avoid how 
many generations of ancestors on the father's side for marriage?
Seven generations.
16184
In modern practice, Maithil Brahmins generally avoid _____ 
generations on the father's side for marriage.
Six generations.
16185
Which historical ruler of the Karnat dynasty is credited with the 
formal organisation of the Panji system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
16186
In which year (Shaka era and AD) was the Panji system officially 
instituted?
Shaka $1248$ (equivalent to $1326$ AD).
16187
Who was the first person appointed to manage the Panji records for 
Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
16188
Who was appointed to manage the Panji records for the Karna 
Kayastha community?
Shankaradutta.
16189
Who was appointed to manage the Panji records for the Kshatriya 
community?
Vijayadutta.
16190
The formal document issued by a Panjikar to certify marriage 
eligibility is called a _____.
Siddhanta Patra (or Asvajana Patra).
16191
The term 'Beeji Purush' refers to what in the context of a Maithil 
lineage?
The earliest known progenitor or ancestor of a specific 'Mul' 
(lineage).
16192
What is the collective term for the 16 specific ancestral relations 
checked to ensure no overlap between bride and groom?
Chait (or Shadvidha).
16193
Gotras that claim three sage ancestors for their Vedic rituals are 
classified as _____.
Tri-pravara.
16194
Gotras that claim five sage ancestors for their Vedic rituals are 
classified as _____.
Panch-pravara.
16195
Maithil Brahmins follow which two primary Vedas?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina branch) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
branch).
16196
Followers of the Yajurveda branch in Mithila are specifically 
known as _____.
Vajasaneyi.
16197
Followers of the Samaveda branch in Mithila are specifically 
known as _____.
Chandoga.
16198
In 'Uderh', a complete introduction requires the identification of 
ancestors from how many total 'Kulas' (lineages)?
$32$
16199
The earliest mention of 'Samuh Lekhya' (collective records) used to 
protect lineage was by which philosopher?
Kumarila Bhatta (in Tantra Varttika).
16200
What specific genealogical error did Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya 
commit that led to the formalisation of the Panji system?
He unknowingly married a close relative (his sister's daughter's 
daughter).
16201
Which Maithil King initiated the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) to 
distinguish between higher and lower social grades?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
16202
Term: 'Mulgram'
Definition: The original village where the 'Beeji Purush' 
(progenitor) of a family resided.
16203
How many main 'Muls' (primary lineages) are currently identified 
within the Panji system?
$35$ (originally $34$ plus the Phandah branch).
16204
According to the Panji system, the progeny of an ineligible 
marriage (Swajana) was historically classified as _____.
Chandal (outcaste).
16205
What is the primary difference in marriage rituals between 
Samavedic (Chandoga) and Yajurvedic (Vajasaneyi) Brahmins in 
Mithila?
Chandogas perform the marriage in two parts (Purva and Uttara), 
whereas Vajasaneyis have no such division.
16206
What must be confirmed about the bride's relationship to the 
groom's maternal uncle?
She must not be the child of the groom's mother's brother.
16207
What are the names of the seven most prominent and well-
established Gotras in Maithil Brahmin society?
Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, Bharadvaja, and 
Katyayana.
16208
Which Panji type specifically records the flaws or 'kshana' 
(degradations) that have occurred in a lineage?
Dushan Panji.
16209
Which Panji type acts as a genealogical map, linking branches of 
families across different villages?
Shakha Panji.
16210
To preserve Panji manuscripts, they should be dried when the sun 
enters which zodiac sign?
Leo (Simha Rashi), usually between mid-August and mid-
September.
16211
What three things are Panji manuscripts explicitly protected from 
according to the traditional verse?
Water (Jala), Oil (Taila), and loose binding (Shithila Bandhana).
16212
The 'Panch Gauda' division of Brahmins includes which five 
regions?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16213
Which mountain range historically divides the 'Panch Gauda' 
Brahmins from the 'Panch Dravida' Brahmins?
Vindhyachal Mountains.
16214
In the context of marriage eligibility, the 'Panchami' rule refers to 
avoiding five generations on which side?
The mother's side.
16215
The 'Saptami' rule in marriage eligibility refers to avoiding seven 
generations on which side?
The father's side.
16216
Which scholar's 'Siddhanta' (rule) regarding father-side generational 
limits was eventually accepted by the Maithil community?
Mahamahopadhyaya Mahesh Thakur.
16217
True or False: The Panji system is currently actively maintained for 
Maithil Brahmins, Karna Kayasthas, and Rajputs.
False (it has largely disappeared among Rajputs).
16218
A 'Panjikar' serves as the equivalent of a modern _____ in the 
context of lineage tracking.
Registrar or Genealogist.
16219
What is the historical significance of 'Tirhuta' script in relation to 
the Panji system?
It is the original script in which all Panji records are written and 
preserved.
16220
Concept: 'Beeji'
Definition: The foundational ancestor from whom a specific 'Mul' 
(original lineage) originates. Example: Sandilya for the 'Dighwa' 
Mul.
16221
The first 'Chait' relation checked is the bride's relationship to which 
ancestor of her father?
The father's great-grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamaha).
16222
What is the primary script used for the digitisation and 
transliteration project mentioned in the source?
Devanagari (transliterated from Tirhuta).
16223
Gotra: 'Bharadvaja'
Definition: One of the 20 Maithil Gotras, belonging to the Tri-
pravara category with ancestors Bharadvaja, Angiras, and 
Barhaspatya.
16224
Gotra: 'Vatsa'
Definition: One of the 20 Maithil Gotras, belonging to the Panch-
pravara category with five distinct sage ancestors.
16225
In the Panji system, if a person's name is recorded in the official 
registers, they are known as _____.
Panjibaddha.
16226
Which Panji type is specifically used to verify the 'Sapinda' (shared 
blood/body) relationship?
Uderh Panji.
16227
What historical event led to the creation of the 'Siddhanta Patra' 
requirement?
The realisation that people were forgetting their lineages, leading to 
unintentional prohibited marriages.
16228
Maithil Brahmins of the 'Chandoga' (Samavedic) tradition must see 
which star after their wedding ceremony?
Arundhati (Tara).
16229
The historical region of 'Mithila' is situated between the Himalayas 
and which river?
The Ganges (Ganga).
16230
Which document is considered the 'Life Force' (Pranadhara) of the 
Tirhuta script?
The Panji Prabandh records.
16231
According to the Panji, the Gotra 'Kashyapa' is associated with 
which three Pravara sages?
Kashyapa, Avatsara, and Naidhruva.
16232
The Panji records identify how many sub-categories or grades 
among Shrotriya Brahmins?
Eight grades (including the 'Chauralla' system).
16233
What is the 'Laukika' in the context of the Panji system?
The specific lineage records or titles associated with the Shrotriya 
category.
16234
How many prohibited 'Chait' relations involve the maternal side of 
the bride?
Eight (from the $9$th to the $16$th 'Chait').
16235
The Panji records are traditionally written on which material?
Palm leaves (Talpatra) or handmade paper (Basaha).
16236
In what year was the Panji Prabandh (genealogy management 
system) of Mithila formally established?
1326 AD (Saka Era 1248).
16237
Which ruler of the Karnata dynasty initiated the systematic 
recording of Maithil lineages?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
16238
What was the primary motive for creating the Panji system in 
Mithila?
To ensure caste purity and strictly follow marriage eligibility rules 
(avoiding prohibited relationships).
16239
Who was the scholar appointed by Maharaja Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Panji for Maithil Brahmins?
Sadupadhyaya Gunaakar Jha.
16240
Which social groups in Mithila originally maintained lineage 
records under the Panji Prabandh?
Brahmins, Kayasthas (Karn Kayastha), and Kshatriyas.
16241
List the four hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins created 
through the Panji system.
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh (Vamshaj), and Jaywar.
16242
In Maithil Brahmin society, what defines the 'Jaywar' category?
Lineages that were not registered or indexed during the initial Panji 
Prabandh.
16243
How many total 'Gotras' (clans) are identified within the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
20
16244
Name the seven most prominent and populous Gotras among 
Maithil Brahmins.
Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, Bharadwaja, and 
Katyayana.
16245
What does the term 'Mula' represent in the context of the Panji 
system?
The original ancestral village or 'root' of a specific clan.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16246
What is the total number of 'Mula Grams' (original villages) 
identified in the Panji records?
167
16247
Define 'Beeji Purush' in Maithil genealogy.
The earliest known progenitor or 'seed' ancestor of a specific 
clan/lineage.
16248
How many primary 'Mulas' (roots) were determined as the most 
significant during the 1326 AD classification?
34 (later expanded to 35).
16249
In the Panji system, the term 'Pravara' refers to _____
The illustrious Vedic ancestors (Rishis) associated with a Gotra.
16250
What is the difference between 'Tripravar' and 'Panchapravar' 
Gotras?
Tripravar Gotras have 3 designated Vedic ancestors, while 
Panchapravar have 5.
16251
Which three Gotras are classified as 'Panchapravar'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharv.
16252
Why is marriage prohibited between individuals of the Vatsa and 
Savarna Gotras despite being distinct names?
They share the same Vedic ancestors (Pravaras).
16253
Maithil Brahmins belong to which two primary Vedic branches 
(Shakhas)?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina branch) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
branch).
16254
Brahmins following the Madhyandina branch of the Yajurveda are 
known as _____
Vajasaneyi.
16255
Brahmins following the Kauthuma branch of the Samaveda are 
known as _____
Chhandogya.
16256
According to traditional Maithil marriage rules, how many 
generations must be excluded from the father's side?
7 generations (paternal side).
16257
According to traditional Maithil marriage rules, how many 
generations must be excluded from the mother's side?
5 generations (maternal side).
16258
Which historical figure modified the paternal exclusion rule to 6 
generations for Maithil society?
Mahamaho-upadhyaya Maharaja Mahesh Thakur.
16259
What is the 'Utedh' in Maithil genealogy?
A detailed pedigree chart checking 32 ancestral branches to verify 
marriage eligibility.
16260
What is the function of a 'Panjikar'?
A specialist genealogist who maintains records and verifies if a 
couple is 'Asvajan' (unrelated) for marriage.
16261
What is the 'Siddhant Patra' issued by a Panjikar?
A formal permit confirming that no prohibited degree of kinship 
(Sapinda) exists between a bride and groom.
16262
The 16 specific ancestral positions that must be checked for 
prohibited matches are called _____
Chhaidht.
16263
Under what condition is a match permitted if a 'Chhaidht' 
(prohibited ancestor) is shared?
If the shared ancestor appears in the groom's maternal line (Matru-
paksh).
16264
Which group of Brahmins is categorised as 'Panch-Gaud'?
Utkal, Kanyakubja, Gaud, Maithil, and Saraswat.
16265
Which group of Brahmins is categorised as 'Panch-Dravid'?
Gurjar, Tailang, Dravid, Karnat, and Maharashtriya.
16266
What is the significance of the Vindhyachal Mountains in Brahmin 
classification?
They serve as the boundary between the Panch-Gaud (North) and 
Panch-Dravid (South) groups.
16267
Which script was traditionally used to record the Panji manuscripts? Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
16268
Term: 'Dushana Panji'
Definition: A record detailing 'flaws' or deviations from caste rules, 
such as irregular marriages, within a lineage.
16269
What was the purpose of 'Sakha Panjis' introduced later by 
Maharaja Madhav Singh?
To provide more detailed branch records for different hierarchical 
sub-categories like Shrotriya.
16270
What is a 'Laukik' in the Shrotriya Panji system?
The specific social standing or honorific status of a family based on 
its lineage history.
16271
Which Chhandogya marriage ritual involves looking at the star 
Arundhati?
Uttar Vivah (performed after sunset).
16272
How does the Panji system relate to modern 'Genome Mapping' in 
the author's theory?
It is viewed as an ancient, systematic method of tracking genetic 
heritage and biological lineage.
16273
What is the 'Asapinda' rule in Hindu marriage law mentioned in the 
text?
The prohibition of marriage between individuals who share a 
common ancestor within a certain number of generations.
16274
Which Maithil Brahmin category is often referred to as 'Jaywar'?
The general class of Brahmins who do not belong to the Shrotriya, 
Yogya, or Panjibaddh grades.
16275
Who were the record-keepers for the Kshatriya clans in Mithila?
Vijayadatta and his descendants.
16276
According to the text, what happened to the offspring of an 
'irregular' marriage involving a non-Brahmin?
The lineage was documented in the 'Dushana Panji' to track the 
deviation from caste purity.
16277
What is 'Samuha Lekhya'?
Collective written records of clan lineages, mentioned by Kumarila 
Bhatt in the 7th century.
16278
Which Mula belongs to the 'Vatsa' Gotra and the village 'Karmahe'?
Karmahe.
16279
In the Gotra 'Kashyapa', which Mula is associated with 'Mandi'?
Mandi.
16280
Which Mula of the 'Shandilya' Gotra is associated with the 'Sarisab' 
lineage?
Sarisab.
16281
The 'Panchapravar' list for the Vatsa Gotra includes Aurva, 
Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and _____
Apnuvana.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16282
What was the role of the King of Mithila in the Panji system?
The King acted as the ultimate authority and protector of the Panji 
records and rules.
16283
Why did Panjikars traditionally sun-dry the manuscripts during the 
month when the Sun enters Leo?
To preserve the palm leaves from moisture and pests using the 
intense heat of that season.
16284
Term: 'Beeji'
Definition: The foundational ancestor of a specific 'Mula' (root) 
from whom the entire branch descends.
16285
Which Panji type specifically records the names of female 
ancestors?
The Panjis of Mithila and Alariya (Maduat-Alariya).
16286
How did regional migration affect the Maithil Brahmin identity 
according to the text?
It led to cultural variations in language, diet, and dress, leading to 
the classification of Brahmins by region (e.g., Maithil, Saraswat).
16287
What is the 'Udhvardhi' division in Mithila?
A vertical division of Brahmins based on their specific Vedic 
Samhita (Vajasaneyi vs Chhandogya).
16288
Identify the primary source of 'Dushana' (defects) mentioned in the 
15th-20th examples.
Marriages or unions with individuals of 'Unknown' (Ajnaat) 
lineages or different castes.
16289
Who was 'Modanand Jha' in the history of Panji Shastra?
A 20th-century scholar and Panjikar who received high academic 
titles for his mastery of lineage records.
16290
The 'Utedh' system ensures that a person is not a _____ of their 
prospective spouse.
Sapinda (consanguineous relative).
16291
What script is used in the modern digital documentation of Panji 
records as per the source?
Devanagari (for transliteration from the original Tirhuta).
16292
In the context of the Panji, what does 'Transliteration' refer to?
The process of converting records from the ancient Tirhuta script to 
modern Devanagari script.
16293
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Katyayana' lineage in Mithila?
Katyayana.
16294
The Samavedi Brahmins of Mithila strictly follow the _____ branch 
(Shakha).
Kauthuma.
16295
The Yajurvedi Brahmins of Mithila strictly follow the _____ branch 
(Shakha).
Madhyandina.
16296
What is 'Vamshavali'?
A chronological family tree or lineage record.
16297
According to the text, why is the Panji system considered 'Unique' 
(Advitiya)?
Because it contains a continuous and detailed record of thousands 
of families over nearly 700 to 1,000 years.
16298
What was the 'Tantra Varttik's' view on lineage protection?
It stated that noble families protect their lineage with great effort 
through written group records (Samuha Lekhya).
16299
Which Maithil Brahmin category is often described as 'Pachhwari-
paat'?
The non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).
16300
Which group is referred to as 'Pubari-paat'?
The Shrotriya Brahmins.
16301
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' in Mithila 
tradition?
To maintain caste purity and regulate marriage eligibility for 
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
16302
During which historical period was the 'Panji Prabandh' formally 
established?
The late medieval period, specifically around 1326 AD (Shaka 
1248).
16303
Who was the Karnat dynasty ruler responsible for formalising the 
Panji system?
Maharaja Harisinghdev.
16304
Term: Udegh
The detailed introduction of 32 ancestors (maternal and paternal) 
required to verify marriage eligibility.
16305
How many generations must be avoided for a marriage to be valid 
on the paternal side according to 'Shrotriya' rules?
Seven generations.
16306
On the maternal side, how many generations must be avoided for a 
valid marriage in the Panji system?
Five generations.
16307
What term is used for Maithil Brahmins who are not registered in 
the Panji records?
Jaibar.
16308
Concept: Biji Purush
The earliest known ancestor from whom a specific lineage or 'Mool' 
originates.
16309
How many 'Gotras' are identified among Maithil Brahmins in the 
Panji records?
20
16310
Maithil Brahmins following the Yajurveda belong to the _____ 
branch.
Madhyandini (or Vajasaneyi).
16311
What are the two main types of 'Pravara' families in the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
Tri-pravara (three ancestors) and Panch-pravara (five ancestors).
16312
The 'Pravara' ancestors are defined as those who originally 
composed the _____.
Rigveda hymns (Suktas).
16313
Which three Gotras are specifically classified as 'Panch-pravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj (though the text also lists Arva).
16314
The document issued by a Panjikar certifying that a couple is not 
'Swajan' (related) is called a _____.
Siddhanta Patra (or Aswajan Patra).
16315
What was the role of Pandit Gunakar Jha in the Panji system?
He was the first Panjikar appointed by Maharaja Harisinghdev to 
manage Brahmin records.
16316
Who was appointed to manage the Panji records for the 'Karna 
Kayastha' community?
Shankardatt.
16317
Term: Moolgram
The original village or ancestral home of a specific lineage.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16318
According to the 'Pancha Gauda' classification, Mithila is one of the 
five Brahmin regions north of the _____.
Vindhyachal Mountains.
16319
The classification of Maithil Brahmins into Shrotriya, Yogya, and 
Panjibaddha occurred during the reign of _____.
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
16320
What is the primary difference in the 'Upanayana' (sacred thread) 
ceremony between Chhandog and Vajasaneyi Brahmins?
Chhandogs have four rituals (Name, Tonsure, Upanayana, 
Samavartan), while Vajasaneyis add Vedrambh.
16321
In Chhandog tradition, when must the 'Uttara Vivah' (latter part of 
the wedding) take place?
After sunset, following the sighting of the star Arundhati.
16322
The concept of 'Genome Mapping' in the text refers to the 1,500-
year-old tradition of _____.
Panji Prabandh (genealogical record keeping).
16323
What term is used for the 16 specific maternal/paternal nodes used 
to check marriage compatibility?
Chhatth.
16324
Which philosopher's work, 'Tantra Varttik', is cited as an early 
reference for 'Samuh Lekhya' (lineage records)?
Kumarila Bhatta.
16325
Why is marriage prohibited between members of the 'Vatsa' and 
'Savarna' Gotras?
Because they share the same 'Biji' ancestors.
16326
Term: Dushan Panji
A specific record used to document historical social or lineage 
'defects' within a family tree.
16327
How many 'Mools' (lineages) were initially identified in the records 
of Maithil Brahmins?
167
16328
The 'Laukik' category of Panji records is primarily associated with 
the _____ hierarchy.
Shrotriya.
16329
Under which conditions did 'Panji' records historically record 
marriages with other castes (e.g. 'Yavana' or 'Chamarkar')?
In the 'Dushan' or 'Upa-Panji' to track the resulting genealogical 
status of descendants.
16330
How many 'Gotras' are considered 'super-eminent' or most populous 
in Maithil society?
Seven (Sandilya, Vatsa, Kashyap, Savarna, Parashar, Bharadwaj, 
and Katyayan).
16331
What is the significance of the 'Sapanpinda' rule in Hindu marriage 
law mentioned in the text?
It prohibits marriage between individuals who share common 
ancestors within defined generational limits.
16332
The '1st Chhatth' refers to the lineage of the bride's _____.
Paternal Great-Great-Grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamaha).
16333
The '9th Chhatth' involves the lineage of the bride's _____.
Maternal Grandfather's Paternal Great-Grandfather.
16334
Which branch of Brahmins is also known as 'Pachhwari Paanth'?
Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddha).
16335
What is the function of the 'Mool Panji'?
It provides a general record of all lineages and their ancestral 
origins.
16336
Which Panji record details the specific branches that emerged from 
a main lineage?
Shakha Panji.
16337
Concept: Siddhanta
The formal confirmation by a Panjikar that a proposed marriage 
does not violate scriptural or genealogical rules.
16338
What is the '16th Chhatth' calculated from?
The Maternal Grandmother's Maternal Grandmother's father.
16339
Maithil Brahmins are a sub-group of the larger _____ division of 
Indian Brahmins.
Pancha Gauda.
16340
Why is the preservation of Panji books (palm leaves) advised 
against lion/sunlight damage in the text?
To protect the historical 'Genome Map' of the community for future 
generations.
16341
Term: Sapanpinda Nirvritti
The point at which two families are far enough removed 
genealogically to allow marriage.
16342
How does the text define a 'Shrotriya' Brahmin in the context of the 
Panji hierarchy?
The highest tier, characterised by strict adherence to rituals and 
genealogical purity.
16343
The '20th Gotra' mentioned in the list is _____.
Jatukarna.
16344
What is the historical significance of the year 1248 Shaka?
It marks the formal introduction of the Panji management system in 
Mithila.
16345
Which professional is tasked with maintaining 'Genome Mapping' 
accuracy in the Panji system?
The Panjikar.
16346
How does 'Tri-pravara' differ from 'Panch-pravara' regarding 
ancestors?
Tri-pravara claims three Vedic sage ancestors, while Panch-pravara 
claims five.
16347
If a girl falls within the prohibited generations of a groom's paternal 
side, she is considered _____.
Swajan (a relative unsuitable for marriage).
16348
The text compares the Panji system to modern _____ science.
Genetic/Genome Mapping.
16349
What is the 'Udegh' of a groom compared to the bride's?
The groom also requires a 32-mool verification (his paternal and 
maternal lines) to ensure no overlap.
16350
In the Maithil Panji, the 'Mool' identifies the clan, while the 
'Moolgram' identifies the _____.
Ancestral village.
16351
Which ancestor is the '13th Chhatth' based on?
The Maternal Grandmother's Paternal Great-Grandfather.
16352
What historical reason does the text give for Brahmins becoming 
distinct based on regions (e.g. Maithil, Utkal)?
Regional differences in climate, language, diet, and local cultures 
over time.
16353
What was the consequence of Pandit Harinath Upadhyay's 
accidental 'Swajan' marriage in the 14th century?
It triggered the formalisation of the Panji system to prevent such 
errors.
16354
The 'Gotra' Sandilya is associated with which 'Pravara' sages?
Sandilya, Asit, and Deval.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16355
Which branch of knowledge does the text claim the Panji system 
anticipated by centuries?
Chromosomal and genetic inheritance patterns.
16356
How many primary 'Mools' are selected as 'Antya Shrestha' (Top 
Grade) in the Panji?
13 (e.g. Khadaure, Khauade, Bodhabade).
16357
The 'Gotra' system in Mithila is _____ (paternal/maternal) in its 
descent pattern.
Patrilineal (Pitra-Pradhan).
16358
Term: Aswajan Patra
A document proving a couple are not related, allowing a wedding to 
proceed.
16359
How is the '6th Chhatth' calculated?
From the Paternal Father's Maternal Grandfather's Maternal 
Grandfather.
16360
What is the 'Dushan' record's role in the 'Genome Mapping' process? To prevent the 'pollution' of lineages by documenting where 
prohibited or non-caste marriages occurred.
16361
Maithil Brahmins are divided into two Vedic groups: Samavedis 
and _____.
Yajurvedis.
16362
The 'Shrotriya' group is divided into _____ (categories) in their 
Panji records.
Chaupala (or categories of hierarchy).
16363
Which Gotra includes the sages Aurva, Chyavana, and Bhargava in 
its Pravara?
Vatsa (and Savarna).
16364
A Panjikar verifies compatibility by checking _____ generations for 
the groom and bride.
Six paternal and five maternal.
16365
The '10th Chhatth' refers to the bride's _____.
Great-Maternal Grandmother's Maternal Grandfather.
16366
The formal collection of Panji records began on the Shaka date 
_____.
1248
16367
Who was the 'Biji Purush' of the Simhashram lineage?
Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
16368
Which 'Mool' is famously associated with the poet Vidyapati?
Bishphi.
16369
In the Panji logic, a marriage is prohibited if the bride is the _____ 
descendant of any of the 16 paternal/maternal nodes of the groom.
Sixth.
16370
What happened to the Panji system for the Kshatriya (Rajput) 
community in Mithila?
It largely fell into disuse or became extinct over time, unlike the 
Brahmin records.
16371
Term: Siddhanta Patra
The certificate of marriage permission issued by the royal Panjikar.
16372
What is the significance of '167 Mools' in the text?
It represents the total number of distinct Brahmin lineages tracked 
in Mithila.
16373
According to the text, 'Swajan' marriage results in progeny being 
considered _____.
Chandal (or socially excluded).
16374
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandha' system in 
Mithila?
To maintain genealogical records for ensuring caste purity and 
determining marriage eligibility.
16375
Which three social groups were originally included in the Panji 
Prabandha system?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
16376
According to the Panji system, what is the 'Uteh'?
A detailed introduction of 32 ancestors from both the paternal and 
maternal sides used to verify marriage eligibility.
16377
In the Maithil Brahmin Panji system, how many generations must 
be avoided on the paternal side for a valid marriage?
Seven generations (often modified to six in later practice).
16378
In the Maithil Brahmin Panji system, how many generations must 
be avoided on the maternal side for a valid marriage?
Five generations.
16379
What are the four main social categories of Maithil Brahmins 
defined by the Panji records?
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh, and Jaibar.
16380
The term used for a Maithil Brahmin who is not registered in the 
formal Panji system is _____.
Jaibar
16381
What is the total number of 'Gotras' identified within the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
20
16382
Which two Vedic branches (Vedas) are primarily followed by 
Maithil Brahmins?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina Shakha) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
Shakha).
16383
Maithil Brahmins following the Samaveda are known as _____.
Chandoga
16384
What is the total number of 'Mools' (original root villages) recorded 
in the Panji records?
167
16385
Out of the 167 original villages, how many are considered 'Mukhya 
Mool' (primary roots)?
34 (later increased to 35 with the inclusion of the Khauwal branch 
of the Phandah mool).
16386
Term: Bijipurush
Definition: The earliest known founding ancestor of a specific 
lineage or 'Mool'.
16387
Which philosopher mentioned 'Samuha Lekhya' (group records) in 
the 7th Century to prove caste purity?
Kumarila Bhatta in his work 'Tantra Varttika'.
16388
The formalisation of the Panji system occurred under which King 
of Mithila?
King Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
16389
In what year (AD) was the Panji Prabandha formally established 
following the assembly of scholars?
1326 AD (Saka 1248).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16390
What historical event triggered King Harisimhadeva to formalise 
genealogical records?
The accidental marriage of the scholar Pandit Harinath Upadhyay to 
a close maternal relative.
16391
Term: Panjikar
Definition: A state-appointed genealogist responsible for 
maintaining Panji records and verifying marriage eligibility.
16392
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Aswajanya Patra)?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride 
and groom are not prohibited relatives.
16393
What are the two types of 'Pravaras' found among Maithil 
Brahmins?
Tri-pravara (three sage ancestors) and Panch-pravara (five sage 
ancestors).
16394
Which specific ancestor is common to both the 'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' 
gotras, preventing marriage between them?
They share the same lineage of five sage ancestors (Aurva, 
Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana).
16395
The list of 10 Brahmin divisions in India classifies Maithils under 
which broader category?
Panch-Gauda (the five northern groups).
16396
Which geographical feature divides the 'Panch-Gauda' Brahmins 
from the 'Panch-Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
16397
How many 'Gotras' are found among the Karn Kayasthas of Mithila? One (Kashyap).
16398
The 16 prohibited genealogical positions for a bride are collectively 
known as _____.
Chhatth
16399
If a bride's 'Chhatth' (prohibited ancestor) appears in the groom's 
_____ side, the marriage is prohibited.
Paternal (Pitripaksh)
16400
If a bride's 'Chhatth' appears in the groom's _____ side, the 
marriage is permitted (after the 5th generation).
Maternal (Matripaksh)
16401
What is 'Dushana Panji'?
A genealogical record that specifically tracks any flaws or historical 
transgressions in a family's lineage.
16402
What is the difference between 'Mool' and 'Moolgram'?
Mool is the original ancestral village, while Moolgram is the 
specific village where the lineage resided during the Panji's 
formalisation.
16403
Which Panjikar was initially appointed for the Brahmin community 
by King Harisimhadeva?
Gunaikar Jha.
16404
Which Panjikar was initially appointed for the Karn Kayastha 
community?
Shankardatta.
16405
Which Panjikar was initially appointed for the Kshatriya 
community?
Vijayadatta.
16406
What is the significance of the month of 'Leo' (Sinha Rashi) for 
Panji preservation?
It is the recommended time to dry and air out the Panji manuscripts 
to protect them from moisture and pests.
16407
The classification 'Pachhwari-paat' refers to which group of Maithil 
Brahmins?
The non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).
16408
The classification 'Purbari-paat' refers to which group of Maithil 
Brahmins?
The Shrotriya Brahmins.
16409
Which Sanskrit work provided the verse detailing the Saka year 
1019 as the foundation for Nanyadeva's rule in Mithila?
The introductory verses of the Panji records.
16410
Name one of the seven primary, most populous gotras of Maithil 
Brahmins.
Sandilya (others include Vatsa, Kashyap, Savarna, Parashara, 
Bharadwaj, and Katyayan).
16411
According to the rule 'Matritah panchami, pitritah saptami', how 
many maternal generations must be avoided?
Five generations.
16412
In the calculation of marriage eligibility, the bride must not be the 
daughter of the groom's _____.
Maternal uncle (Maima).
16413
Who was the author of 'Smritisar', whose personal genealogical 
error led to the Panji reform?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyay.
16414
Term: Shakha Panji
Definition: A specialized genealogical book documenting the 
branches and status changes of families, particularly Shrotriyas.
16415
How many categories of Shrotriya Brahmins are currently identified 
in the records?
Eight (known as Chauwala or Chaurayil).
16416
How many Shrotriya Panjis are currently in existence?
32
16417
How many Panjis exist for the Yogya and Panjibaddh categories 
combined?
153
16418
Concept: Adhogami
Definition: The downward social mobility of a lineage's status due 
to marriages into lower-ranked families.
16419
In the list of 32 ancestral roots (Uteh), the 5th 'Chhatth' refers to the 
bride's _____.
Paternal grandmother's great-grandfather.
16420
Which scripts were traditionally used to write the Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
16421
Why is the Savarna gotra prohibited from marrying into the Vatsa 
gotra?
Because they share identical 'Pravaras' (sage ancestors).
16422
Which specific ancestor is the 'Bijipurush' of the Simhashram Mool? Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
16423
The 10th 'Chhatth' in the genealogical search refers to the bride's 
_____.
Great-grandfather's maternal grandfather (Pramatamaha).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
16424
Under whose reign did the 'Shakha Panji' and the rigid hierarchy of 
Shrotriyas begin?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
16425
Which type of Panji record tracks historical ' Kshatran' or specific 
family status changes?
Dushana Panji.
16426
The terminology 'Laukik' is primarily associated with which group's 
genealogy?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
16427
What is the 'Aswajan-yapar' Panji?
A record detailing descendants from inter-caste or prohibited 
marriages.
16428
In the context of Panji, what does 'Chhatth' literally mean in the 
calculation of prohibited degrees?
The sixth position/generation in a specific genealogical line.
16429
Which Sanskrit work is cited to define the Sapinda relationship as 
'five from the mother and seven from the father'?
Manusmriti.
16430
The Panji records identify Maithils following the Vajasaneyi branch 
as followers of which Veda?
Yajurveda.
16431
Which specific scholar's lineage is traced back to Tripurari Bhatta 
(c. 400 AD) in the Panji records?
Aryabhatta.
16432
The primary duty of a Panjikar before a marriage is to verify that 
the couple are _____.
Asapinda (not related within prohibited degrees).
16433
In what year was the Panji Prabandh system established in Mithila 
according to the source?
$1326$ AD (Saka $1248$).
16434
Which Karnata dynasty ruler is credited with the formalisation of 
the Maithil Panji system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
16435
The Maithil Panji system was primarily created to maintain the 
purity of which three castes?
Brahmin, Kayastha, and Kshatriya.
16436
What is the term for the introduction of seven generations of 
ancestors required for marriage eligibility?
Udhedh.
16437
According to the Panji system, how many maternal ancestors must 
be introduced for an 'Udhedh'?
Five generations.
16438
According to the Panji system, how many paternal ancestors must 
be introduced for an 'Udhedh'?
Seven generations.
16439
Which Maithil Brahmin group is designated as 'Jaywar'?
Those who were not registered in the formal Panji records.
16440
In the modern Panji system, what is the required generation gap to 
be discarded on the father's side for marriage?
Seven generations (modified to six in later practices).
16441
In the modern Panji system, what is the required generation gap to 
be discarded on the mother's side for marriage?
Five generations.
16442
The Maithil Brahmins are divided into how many 'Mools' (original 
ancestral villages)?
$167$ Mools.
16443
How many main 'Gotras' are listed for Maithil Brahmins in total?
$20$ Gotras.
16444
Identify the two main Vedic branches (Shakhas) followed by 
Maithil Brahmins.
Yajurveda (Madhyandina) and Samaveda (Kauthuma).
16445
Followers of the Samaveda Kauthuma branch are specifically 
referred to as _____.
Chhandog.
16446
Followers of the Yajurveda Madhyandina branch are specifically 
referred to as _____.
Vajasaneyi.
16447
What is the primary difference in the marriage ceremony of 
Chhandog Maithil Brahmins compared to others?
It is divided into two parts: Purva-Vivah and Uttar-Vivah (after 
seeing the star Arundhati).
16448
What defines a 'Tri-pravara' family in the Maithil Brahmin tradition? They have three ancestors who composed Rigvedic hymns.
16449
What defines a 'Panch-pravara' family in the Maithil Brahmin 
tradition?
They have five ancestors who composed Rigvedic hymns.
16450
Which two Gotras are forbidden from intermarrying because they 
share the same ancestors?
Vatsa and Savarna.
16451
Who was the scholar whose 'Smritisara' text highlighted the danger 
of unintentional incestuous marriage, leading to the Panji system?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
16452
The professional genealogist appointed by the King to maintain 
lineage records is known as a _____.
Panjikar.
16453
Which individual was first tasked by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Brahmin Panji records?
Gunakar Jha.
16454
Which individual was first tasked by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Karna Kayastha Panji records?
Shankardatta.
16455
Which individual was first tasked by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Kshatriya Panji records?
Vijaydatta.
16456
Identify the four social hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
created after the Panji management by Maharaja Madhav Singh.
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh, and Jaywar.
16457
